name: add-policy
tagline: Guides adding, wiring, and exporting VS Code configuration policies across OS, account, and managed-settings channels.
verdict: Thorough, actionable procedure with concrete TypeScript examples, a clear architecture map, and documented failure modes (export-data quirks, CI-only regeneration pitfalls). Two gaps: no explicit out-of-scope guidance, so it could misfire on general (non-policy) settings work (Q02 fail); and no version/changelog signal (H08 fail).

highlights.summary: No 'NOT for' / out-of-scope guidance anywhere; the policy domain is broad enough (OS, account, managed-settings, extension policies) that adjacent non-policy settings work could misfire it.
No version field, changelog section, or dated revision notes anywhere in the file.
